title: lessertia frutescens subsp. frutescens
intro: Lessertia frutescens subsp. frutescens, also known as Sutherland's clover or cancer bush, is a plant species native to South Africa and used in traditional medicine. The plant contains a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to have medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and even cancer, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and extracts, can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
